purpose: Machine-readable triliteral verb measures (forms I-X) + rare/quadriliteral/geminate notes. Drives sarf decisions: pick the form, choose active vs passive wording, gloss a maṣdar nominally, gloss a participle as agent/patient, never read a derived form as Form I.

slot_english.past_passive: it was done (Past, Unknown) — al-māḍī al-majhūl
slot_english.present_active: he is doing (Present/Future, Known) — al-muḍāriʿ al-maʿlūm
slot_english.present_passive: it is (being) done (Present/Future, Unknown) — al-muḍāriʿ al-majhūl
slot_english.masdar: the act of doing (verbal noun) — al-maṣdar
slot_english.ism_fa3il: the one who does / the doer (active participle) — ism al-fāʿil
slot_english.ism_maf3ul: the one/thing done (passive participle) — ism al-mafʿūl

negation.negation_future: لَنْ + subjunctive — 'he will not do' (categorical future negation)
negation.negation_past: لَمْ + jussive — 'he has not / did not do' (negates the past, governs the muḍāriʿ majzūm)
negation.note: لَمْ flips a present-tense FORM to a PAST MEANING; a hover gloss must follow the governing negative, not the surface tense.

forms.sense: the base lexical action; transitivity and vowel of the muḍāriʿ are lexical (learned).
intensive / causative / declarative / denominative. Chart: 'strengthening/intensifying of meaning; applying the act to a more general object'.
associative / conative — doing the act toward another, or attempting it. Chart: 'relation of the action to another person; attempting the act'.
causative — to make someone/something do/become. The hamza changes sense vs Form I; do NOT gloss as Form I.
reflexive/effective of II; to take on / consider oneself to have the quality. Chart: 'to consider/represent oneself as having a quality expressed in the root'.
quran_examples: تَوَفَّى (took in full / caused to die)

forms.sense: reciprocal / mutual; pretending. Chart: 'implying the mutual application of the action'.
mediopassive / anticausative — to become X-ed (intransitive result). No passive (already passive in sense).
reflexive / middle voice; doing for oneself. The infixed ت assimilates after certain first radicals (e.g. اِصْطَبَرَ, اِزْدَادَ).
acquisition of a colour or a bodily defect. Chart: 'possession/acquisition of colours/defects'.

kataba.note: k-t-b does not form IX (semantic restriction to colours/defects)

forms.sense: requestative (to seek/ask for the act) or estimative (to deem X to be ...). Chart: 'asking for the act/quality of the root; esteeming/thinking someone/thing to be ...'.

rare_forms.note: Charts show XI-XV markers labelled 'specialised meaning, very rare in use'.

XI.sense: colours/defects, more intensive than IX (chart wording)

XII_XV.sense: very rare; specialised; not productive in Qurʾānic vocabulary

geminate.note: Chart 6 (model 'to shake' = هَزَّ) — doubled verbs مضاعف where C2=C3; written with shadda when the two identicals meet.
geminate.behaviour: idghām (merging) of the doubled radical; the shadda hides the third radical from norm()-style matching.

provenance.informed_by: Paradigm STRUCTURE + English slot labels + form organisation observed in third-party teaching verb-charts (see corpora/sarfnahw catalogue; licensing under review) and the open-source qutrub conjugator roster.
provenance.authored: All Arabic wazn patterns and example realisations here are standard classical Arabic morphology, authored for this repo — NOT OCR-copied from any chart (the chart Arabic is a non-Unicode legacy font and was not extracted).
provenance.public_safety: These are language-intelligence reference facts. They never ship as Qamus hover gloss text; live hover records remain {src:'qamus',kind:'authored'}.
